# Spec-Driven Development Tooling Landscape
<!-- Generated By: Claude Code (Claude Opus 4.6) -->

Survey of tools, platforms, and integrations relevant to spec-driven AI development workflows and their impact on engineering estimation. Conducted March 2026.

## Summary Table

| Category | Tools Surveyed | Key Finding |
|---|---|---|
| [SDD Platforms](sdd-platforms.md) | Spec-Kit, Kiro, Tessl, OpenSpec | All produce task breakdowns but none produce estimation units natively |
| [IDE Integration](ide-integration.md) | Superpowers, AGENTS.md, Cursor Rules, MCP servers | SDD tooling shifts effort from coding to specification, reinforcing the 16-39% coding share finding |
| [Estimation-Adjacent](estimation-adjacent.md) | CostGPT, Devtimate, CodeScene, Faros AI, LinearB | LLMs achieve only 16% accuracy on story points; better at decomposition than quantification |
| [Gaps & Opportunities](gaps-and-opportunities.md) | Drift detection, brownfield specs, multi-repo coordination | Spec-to-estimation intelligence is the highest-value gap but requires solving five prerequisite gaps first |

## Cross-Cutting Findings

**1. LLMs decompose better than they quantify.** Every SDD platform produces task breakdowns, but none generates reliable effort numbers. Academic experiments show 16% accuracy for LLM story point estimation vs. 80-95% claimed by commercial tools (unvalidated). The evidence supports using AI for task structure while keeping humans on effort numbers.

**2. No tool covers the full spec-to-delivery estimation loop.** The most promising pattern combines spec validation (spec-kit), complexity analysis (CodeScene), and delivery metrics (Faros/LinearB) into a feedback loop — but this integration does not exist as a product.

**3. Spec quality is now measurable, not just subjective.** Spec-kit's `/speckit.checklist` and Innoslate's NLP quality scoring make the estimation methodology's "Context Factor" derivable from tool output rather than human judgment alone.

**4. Drift is the estimation killer.** If specs drift from implementation, estimates derived from specs are based on stale information. Specmatic finds ~25% of bugs are contract mismatches. Only API-boundary drift detection exists today; arbitrary spec-to-code drift detection is unsolved.

**5. Brownfield is the adoption bottleneck.** Most SDD tools assume greenfield. Tessl CLI has basic `document --code`, and OpenSpec has delta markers, but generating specs from existing codebases remains largely manual. This limits estimation methodology applicability to teams that already have specs.

## Estimation Methodology Impact

These findings affect the estimation methodology in several ways — see the "Tooling Impact" section in `research/estimation-methodology.md` for details:

- Task classification (A/B/C/D) can be partially automated via spec completeness checkers
- Specification phase time may shrink with SDD tooling that scaffolds specs
- Rework factor may decrease with drift detection
- The spec-driven SDLC proportions are validated by tooling evidence
